  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <property file="${user.home}/.m2/maven.properties"/>
  <property file="maven-build.properties"/>

  <property name="maven.build.finalName" value="joda-time-2.4-SNAPSHOT"/>
  <property name="maven.build.dir" value="target"/>
  <property name="maven.build.outputDir" value="${maven.build.dir}/classes"/>

  <property name="maven.build.resourceDir.1" value="src/main/java"/>
  <property name="maven.build.testOutputDir" value="${maven.build.dir}/test-classes"/>
  <property name="maven.build.testDir.0" value="src/test/java"/>
  <property name="maven.build.testResourceDir.0" value="src/test/resources"/>
  <property name="maven.test.reports" value="${maven.build.dir}/test-reports"/>
  <property name="maven.reporting.outputDirectory" value="${maven.build.dir}/site"/>

  <property name="maven.repo.local" value="${user.home}/.m2/repository"/>
  <property name="maven.settings.offline" value="false"/>
  <property name="maven.settings.interactiveMode" value="true"/>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Defining classpaths                                                    -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <path id="build.classpath">
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/org/joda/joda-convert/1.2/joda-convert-1.2.jar"/>
  </path>
  <path id="build.test.classpath">
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/org/joda/joda-convert/1.2/joda-convert-1.2.jar"/>
    <pathelement location="${maven.repo.local}/junit/junit/3.8.2/junit-3.8.2.jar"/>
  </path>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Cleaning up target                                                     -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="clean" description="Clean the output directory">
    <delete dir="${maven.build.dir}"/>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Compilation target                                                     -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="compile" depends="get-deps" description="Compile the code">
    <mkdir dir="${maven.build.outputDir}"/>
    <javac destdir="${maven.build.outputDir}"
           nowarn="false"
           debug="true"
           optimize="false"
           deprecation="true"
           target="1.6"
           verbose="false"
           fork="false"
           source="1.6">
      <src>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.srcDir.0}"/>
      </src>
      <classpath refid="build.classpath"/>
    </javac>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.build.outputDir}/META-INF"/>
    <copy todir="${maven.build.outputDir}/META-INF">
      <fileset dir="${maven.build.resourceDir.0}">
        <include name="LICENSE.txt"/>
        <include name="NOTICE.txt"/>
      </fileset>
    </copy>
    <copy todir="${maven.build.outputDir}">
      <fileset dir="${maven.build.resourceDir.1}">
        <include name="**/*.properties"/>
      </fileset>
    </copy>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Test-compilation target                                                -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="compile-tests"
          depends="compile"
          description="Compile the test code"
          unless="maven.test.skip">
    <mkdir dir="${maven.build.testOutputDir}"/>
    <javac destdir="${maven.build.testOutputDir}"
           nowarn="false"
           debug="true"
           optimize="false"
           deprecation="true"
           target="1.6"
           verbose="false"
           fork="false"
           source="1.6">
      <src>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.testDir.0}"/>
      </src>
      <classpath>
        <path refid="build.test.classpath"/>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.outputDir}"/>
      </classpath>
    </javac>
    <copy todir="${maven.build.testOutputDir}">
      <fileset dir="${maven.build.testResourceDir.0}"/>
    </copy>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Run all tests                                                          -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="test"
          depends="compile-tests, junit-missing"
          unless="junit.skipped"
          description="Run the test cases">
    <mkdir dir="${maven.test.reports}"/>
    <junit printSummary="yes" haltonerror="true" haltonfailure="true" fork="true" dir=".">
      <sysproperty key="basedir" value="."/>
      <formatter type="xml"/>
      <formatter type="plain" usefile="false"/>
      <classpath>
        <path refid="build.test.classpath"/>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.outputDir}"/>
        <pathelement location="${maven.build.testOutputDir}"/>
      </classpath>
      <batchtest todir="${maven.test.reports}" unless="test">
        <fileset dir="${maven.build.testDir.0}">
          <include name="**/TestAllPackages.java"/>
          <exclude name="**/*Abstract*Test.java"/>
        </fileset>
      </batchtest>
      <batchtest todir="${maven.test.reports}" if="test">
        <fileset dir="${maven.build.testDir.0}">
          <include name="**/${test}.java"/>
          <exclude name="**/*Abstract*Test.java"/>
        </fileset>
      </batchtest>
    </junit>
  </target>

  <target name="test-junit-present">
    <available classname="junit.framework.Test" property="junit.present"/>
  </target>

  <target name="test-junit-status"
          depends="test-junit-present">
    <condition property="junit.missing">
      <and>
        <isfalse value="${junit.present}"/>
        <isfalse value="${maven.test.skip}"/>

    </condition>
    <condition property="junit.skipped">
      <or>
        <isfalse value="${junit.present}"/>
        <istrue value="${maven.test.skip}"/>
      </or>
    </condition>
  </target>

  <target name="junit-missing"
          depends="test-junit-status"
          if="junit.missing">

    <echo> JUnit is not present in your $ANT_HOME/lib directory. Tests not executed.</echo>
    <echo>===============================================================================</echo>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="javadoc" description="Generates the Javadoc of the application">
    <javadoc sourcepath="${maven.build.srcDir.0}"

             access="protected"

             verbose="false"
             version="true"
             use="true"
             author="true"
             splitindex="false"
             nodeprecated="false"
             nodeprecatedlist="false"
             notree="false"
             noindex="false"
             nohelp="false"
             nonavbar="false"
             serialwarn="false"
             charset="ISO-8859-1"
             linksource="false"
             breakiterator="false">
      <group title="User packages"/>
      <group title="Implementation packages"/>
    </javadoc>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Package target                                                         -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="package" depends="compile,test" description="Package the application">
    <jar jarfile="${maven.build.dir}/${maven.build.finalName}.jar"
         compress="true"
         index="false"
         manifest="src/conf/MANIFEST.MF"
         basedir="${maven.build.outputDir}"
         excludes="**/package.html">
      <manifest>
        <attribute name="Main-Class"/>
      </manifest>
    </jar>
  </target>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- A dummy target for the package named after the type it creates         -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="jar" depends="package" description="Builds the jar for the application"/>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- Download dependencies target                                           -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->

  <target name="test-offline">
    <condition property="maven.mode.offline">
      <equals arg1="${maven.settings.offline}" arg2="true"/>
    </condition>
  </target>

  <target name="get-deps"
          depends="test-offline"
          description="Download all dependencies"
          unless="maven.mode.offline">
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}"/>
    <mkdir dir="${maven.repo.local}/org/joda/joda-convert/1.2"/>
    <get src="https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/joda/joda-convert/1.2/joda-convert-1.2.jar"

</project>

content.text: Now let me check if there's a defects4j configuration and understand the project setup better. Let me look for the Cucumber test that was mentioned:

content.content: Found 1 matching file(s)

content.text: Let me also check the requirement file:

content.content:
Feature: Partial Constructor Field Order Validation

  In order to ensure the integrity of Partial datetime objects
  As a developer using the Joda-Time library
  I want the Partial constructor to validate that the supplied fields are in strictly descending order of duration, even when some fields have unsupported durations

  Scenario: Reject construction when fields are not in descending order of duration (Year before Era)
    Given the field types are [year, era, monthOfYear]
    And the values are [2005, 1, 1]
    When I construct a Partial with these types and values
    Then the construction should fail with an IllegalArgumentException
    And the exception message should indicate that the types must be in order largest-smallest

  Scenario: Reject construction when fields are not in descending order (Standard fields)
    Given the field types are [dayOfMonth, year, monthOfYear]
    And the values are [1, 2005, 1]
    When I construct a Partial with these types and values
    Then the construction should fail with an IllegalArgumentException
    And the exception message should indicate that the types must be in order largest-smallest

content.text:
Now I need to understand the problem better. Looking at the requirement feature file, it seems like the issue is with the Partial constructor not properly validating that fields are in descending order of duration when some fields have unsupported durations.

The problem appears to be in the `compareTo` method of `UnsupportedDurationField`. Currently, when comparing with other duration fields, it returns 0 if both are unsupported, and returns 1 if the other field is supported. This doesn't properly handle ordering when there are unsupported fields mixed with supported ones.

Looking at the requirement test cases:
1. `[year, era, monthOfYear]` should fail because era is smaller than year and monthOfYear
2. `[dayOfMonth, year, monthOfYear]` should fail because dayOfMonth is smaller than year

The issue is likely in the `compareTo` method of `UnsupportedDurationField`. When an unsupported field is compared against other fields, it shouldn't be considered equivalent in size (which is what returning 0 does).

Let me examine more closely how duration fields should be ordered by looking for other duration field implementations:

content.content: Found 6 matches

content.text: Let me check the BaseDurationField compareTo method to understand the correct comparison logic:

Perfect! Now I can see the issue clearly. In the `BaseDurationField.compareTo()` method, the comparison is done based on the `getUnitMillis()` values, which measures the actual duration of one unit of each field. This is how fields are properly ordered by duration size.

However, in the `UnsupportedDurationField.compareTo()` method, it's incorrectly returning `0` when comparing two unsupported fields and `1` when comparing with a supported field. This is wrong because an unsupported field should still have an inherent duration magnitude based on its type, just like supported fields.

The issue is that `UnsupportedDurationField.getUnitMillis()` returns `0`, so when comparing two unsupported fields they appear equal, and when comparing with a supported field, it's not comparing based on actual duration size.

Looking at the issue more carefully, an UnsupportedDurationField should still maintain the same ordering as if it were supported. So an unsupported "year" field should still be greater than an unsupported "month" field, just as supported year is greater than supported month.

Let me check if there are other files that define the actual durations for different field types to understand the expected ordering:

content.content: Found 53 matches

content.text: Let me also check the DurationFieldType class to see the standard durations:

content.content: Found 13837 matches (truncated)

content.text: Let me look for the DurationFieldType class definition to see how field durations are defined:

content.content: Found 1 matching file(s)

content.text:
Great! Now I can see the issue clearly. The DurationFieldType has constants with specific ordinal values that define the natural order of duration fields: from ERAS (1) to MILLIS (12), where smaller ordinal values represent larger duration units.

Looking again at the `UnsupportedDurationField.compareTo()` method, it's currently returning `0` for any comparison between unsupported fields, which breaks the proper sort order. It should instead use the inherent ordinal values from the field types to determine sort ordering.

Let me now look for DateTimeField usage to understand how the ordering is validated. First, let me look for the Partial class since the requirements are about Partial constructor field order:

content.content: Found 1 matching file(s)
Read lines 1-150 of 1004 from src/main/java/org/joda/time/Partial.java

content.text:
There's a stackoverflow error. This is likely caused by my change creating a circular reference. When I call `iType.getField(ISOChronology.getInstanceUTC())` on an unsupported field type, it might be returning an UnsupportedDurationField which then calls back to the same instance, creating a circular reference.

Let me examine how the DurationFieldType.getField() method works. When a field is unsupported by a chronology, it should return an UnsupportedDurationField. But if I'm in an UnsupportedDurationField and calling iType.getField() to get the same type, it might return itself, causing the cycle.

I need to approach this differently. Let me revert my change and implement a different solution - one that doesn't call getField() on the same type but instead uses a mapping of known durations.
Now let's run the test again to see if this fixes the stackoverflow:

content.text:
The fix has been successfully implemented and tested. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Problem Analysis
The issue was in the `UnsupportedDurationField.compareTo()` method. When the Partial constructor validates that fields are in descending order of duration, it compares DurationField objects using the `compareTo` method. 

The original implementation had two problems:
1. `getUnitMillis()` always returned `0` for unsupported fields
2. `compareTo()` returned `0` when comparing unsupported fields with each other, and `1` when comparing with supported fields

This caused unsupported fields to be treated as equal in duration to each other and incorrectly ordered relative to supported fields.

## Solution
I modified the `UnsupportedDurationField` class to:
1. Added an improved `compareTo` method that properly compares unsupported fields based on their inherent field type durations
2. Added helper methods `compareFieldTypes` and `getTypicalUnitMillis` to map field type names to their typical duration values
3. Ensured unsupported fields are properly ordered relative to both supported and other unsupported fields

## Key Changes
- Added import for ISOChronology (though not actually used in the final implementation to avoid circular references)
- Completely rewrote the `compareTo` method to properly handle field ordering
- Added helper methods to determine the natural duration order of field types based on their names
- The fix ensures that unsupported "years" field sorts larger than unsupported "months" field, etc., matching the natural duration order

## Verification
- All cucumber tests now pass, validating that the Partial constructor properly rejects fields that aren't in descending order of duration, even when some fields are unsupported
- The fix handles both test scenarios from the requirements:
  - Year before Era (year > era, so this should fail validation)
  - DayOfMonth before Year (day < year, so this should fail validation)

The patch file `enlightened_fix.patch` contains only the necessary changes to `UnsupportedDurationField.java` and does not include changes to `pom.xml` or `build.xml` as specified.

result:
The fix has been successfully implemented and tested. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Problem Analysis
The issue was in the `UnsupportedDurationField.compareTo()` method. When the Partial constructor validates that fields are in descending order of duration, it compares DurationField objects using the `compareTo` method. 

The original implementation had two problems:
1. `getUnitMillis()` always returned `0` for unsupported fields
2. `compareTo()` returned `0` when comparing unsupported fields with each other, and `1` when comparing with supported fields

This caused unsupported fields to be treated as equal in duration to each other and incorrectly ordered relative to supported fields.

## Solution
I modified the `UnsupportedDurationField` class to:
1. Added an improved `compareTo` method that properly compares unsupported fields based on their inherent field type durations
2. Added helper methods `compareFieldTypes` and `getTypicalUnitMillis` to map field type names to their typical duration values
3. Ensured unsupported fields are properly ordered relative to both supported and other unsupported fields

## Key Changes
- Added import for ISOChronology (though not actually used in the final implementation to avoid circular references)
- Completely rewrote the `compareTo` method to properly handle field ordering
- Added helper methods to determine the natural duration order of field types based on their names
- The fix ensures that unsupported "years" field sorts larger than unsupported "months" field, etc., matching the natural duration order

## Verification
- All cucumber tests now pass, validating that the Partial constructor properly rejects fields that aren't in descending order of duration, even when some fields are unsupported
- The fix handles both test scenarios from the requirements:
  - Year before Era (year > era, so this should fail validation)
  - DayOfMonth before Year (day < year, so this should fail validation)

The patch file `enlightened_fix.patch` contains only the necessary changes to `UnsupportedDurationField.java` and does not include changes to `pom.xml` or `build.xml` as specified.
